summary: Red-team review signal for CPL-02 transcript -> context_bundle assembly. Twelve findings (no S4): nine S2/S3 attack vectors and three S0/S1 hygiene items. All S2+ findings closed in-batch via fix actions CPL02-FIX-001..009 with regression tests (TestRedTeamRegressions, TestReferentialIntegrity, TestReplayDeterminism). This is a non-authority review signal; canonical control ownership remains with CDE/SEL.

findings.description: Missing speaker_turns on the input transcript could silently produce an empty bundle if the assembler did not validate fail-closed ahead of segment construction.
findings.recommendation: Raise MISSING_SPEAKER_TURNS; schema requires segments with minItems: 1.

findings.description: A forged segment with a source_turn_id that does not exist in the transcript could pass schema validation but break lineage.
Segment text could be tampered while keeping the matching source_turn_id, breaking trace fidelity.
findings.recommendation: Compare (speaker, text, line_index) against the source turn at the same index; reject SEGMENT_TURN_DRIFT.

findings.description: Reordering segments would still pass schema validation and silently break replay equivalence.
findings.recommendation: Compute manifest_hash over the ordered manifest; pin artifact_id derivation to that hash so reordering produces a different artifact_id.

findings.description: Partial transcript coverage (segments < turns) would lose information silently.
Duplicate segment_id could collide downstream lookup tables.
findings.recommendation: Reject DUPLICATE_SEGMENT_ID; derive ids deterministically as SEG-NNNN.

findings.description: Duplicate turn_id in the source transcript could produce an ambiguous bundle.
Direct call to ArtifactStore.register_artifact could bypass PQX trace propagation and mint a bundle without an execution_record.
findings.recommendation: The pure assembler returns a payload without content_hash so the store rejects it; only PQX mints the hash. Lock with regression test test_assembler_cannot_write_artifact_directly.

findings.description: Replay non-determinism: different trace_id, run_id, or created_at could perturb manifest_hash / content_hash if canonicalization were sloppy.
findings.recommendation: manifest_hash is computed over segments only; content_hash excludes trace/created_at per hash_utils. Locked by TestReplayDeterminism::test_manifest_hash_independent_of_envelope.

findings.description: trace_id / span_id are validated only by schema; calling the assembler outside PQX could produce a malformed trace silently.
findings.recommendation: Validate trace at the assembler boundary with INVALID_TRACE_ID / INVALID_SPAN_ID reason codes.

findings.description: A non-transcript_artifact input could be admitted and silently produce a bundle.
findings.recommendation: Reject INVALID_SOURCE_ARTIFACT_TYPE and INVALID_SOURCE_ARTIFACT_ID at the envelope check.

findings.description: Module docstring did not state that content_hash is owned by the artifact store and manifest_hash is owned by the assembler.
findings.recommendation: Document the split in the module docstring and the return-value contract.
